page: Microsoft Azure D16lds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dldsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32.0 GB of system memory, translating to a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No GPU is included. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 945 GB, and the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-threaded performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2163.0 and a gzip compression rate of 31.37 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance is solid, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15986.98 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 13256.0. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), support accelerated vector and matrix operations. This is reflected in the top-tier text generation speed of 396.44 tokens per second for small language model inference.

The D16lds_v6 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios. With only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, it is not designed for memory-intensive databases or massive in-memory data processing. However, the inclusion of 945 GB of local storage and high-speed CPU capabilities makes it highly suitable for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, text processing, and light machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D16lds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dldsv6 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance delivers strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2163.0 and a gzip compression rate of 31.37 GB/sec. It also features advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enabling top-tier text generation speeds of 396.44 tokens per second for small language model inference. With a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, this server represents a cost-efficient choice for compute-bound tasks rather than memory-heavy applications. It is well-suited for web serving, software compilation, text processing, and lightweight machine learning inference workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16lds_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16lds_v6 is a 16-vCPU compute-optimized VM with 32.0 GB RAM, 945 GB local storage, and an Intel Xeon 4.2 GHz processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel processors, integrated local storage, and advanced matrix acceleration for efficient text processing.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Strong single-core performance for text processing and software compilation
